diff:
diff --git a/doc/man_docs.go b/doc/man_docs.go
index 560bc20..3f09215 100644
--- a/doc/man_docs.go
+++ b/doc/man_docs.go
@@ -209,6 +209,10 @@ func genMan(cmd *cobra.Command, header *GenManHeader) []byte {
        buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
 
        manPreamble(buf, header, cmd, dashCommandName)
+       if len(cmd.Deprecated) > 0 {
+               buf.WriteString("# DEPRECATED\n")
+               buf.WriteString(cmd.Deprecated + "\n\n")
+       }
        manPrintOptions(buf, cmd)

exploration_log.aha_moment: genMan is the actual content assembler; adding the DEPRECATED section after manPreamble (which writes NAME/SYNOPSIS/DESCRIPTION) follows the man page convention.
exploration_log.hindsight: Nothing to do differently; fix location was clear from reading genMan.

key_decisions.decision: Add DEPRECATED in genMan after manPreamble call, before manPrintOptions
key_decisions.reasoning: genMan assembles all sections sequentially; placing DEPRECATED after the standard preamble (NAME/SYNOPSIS/DESCRIPTION) follows common man page conventions

solve_notes: Added a `# DEPRECATED` section in `genMan` in `doc/man_docs.go`, placed after `manPreamble` (which writes NAME, SYNOPSIS, DESCRIPTION) and before `manPrintOptions`. The section is only written when `cmd.Deprecated` is non-empty, following the man page section convention of uppercase headings.
task_text:
Fix man page doc generator not rendering Deprecated field

When a command has its `Deprecated` field set, the man page generator
in `doc/man_docs.go` omits the deprecation notice entirely. The
`GenManTree` function should include a "DEPRECATED" section in the
generated man page with the deprecation message.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Calls GenMan which calls genMan, covering the changed section-assembly code
Also calls GenMan which delegates to genMan

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of genMan and GenMan in doc/man_docs.go

thrash_preventing_defs.name: manPreamble
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding what manPreamble writes (NAME/SYNOPSIS/DESCRIPTION) is needed to decide where to insert the DEPRECATED section without disrupting the standard man page order

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient_defs contains only genMan (edited). thrash_preventing adds manPreamble so an agent understands what comes before the insertion point and can place DEPRECATED in the right order without re-searching.
